title: Lithocarpus elephantum
taxonomy: Lithocarpus elephantum is classified within the Fagaceae family, which includes various types of oak and beech trees. This species belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, a group of trees known as stone oaks or related species. Lithocarpus elephantum is a large evergreen tree that is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries like India and Sri Lanka. The classification of Lithocarpus elephantum is further supported by its distinct morphological features, such as its large leaves and robust trunk. The tree's classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary history and ecological role in its native habitat.
uses: Lithocarpus elephantum, also known as the giant Burmese walnut, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its bark is also used as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help protect against infections and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of Lithocarpus elephantum's medicinal uses.
benefits: Lithocarpus elephantum has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, and research suggests that it may have several health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which could help in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Additionally, it is said to have antiviral properties, making it potentially useful in combating viral infections. The plant is also thought to have antipyretic properties, which may help in reducing fever. However, more research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.

intro: Lithocarpus elephantum, also known as the elephant pod or Burmese walnut, is a species of deciduous tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus elephantum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus elephantum is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
